title: Aves
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|[[Talon Karrde]]'s aide|[[Aves (general)|Aves]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[General/Legends|general]]}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:AvesBlonde.jpg]] |name=Aves |homeworld= |birth=Prior to [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]]''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' states that Aves was over forty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old during the events of ''[[Heir to the Empire]]'', which take place five years after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Since the battle is dated to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', Aves was born prior to [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]]. |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=1.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blond]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Fair |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Smugglers' Alliance]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Don't remember me, huh?''"
"''Should I?''"
"''I'd have thought so. You go up against a Star Destroyer with someone, he ought to remember you. Especially out in the middle of nowhere''.|Aves and Wedge Antilles|''[[The Last Command]]''}} '''Aves''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who served as the chief [[Aide/Legends|aide]] and communications officer for the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]]. Having worked with Karrde since the early days of his organization, Aves was Karrde's spokesman for dealings with [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. ==Biography== [[File:Aves.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Aves.]] Aves was early middle-aged when he joined Karrde's crew, shortly after the death of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]{{GamerCite|2|A Credit for Your Thoughts}}. From the early days of Karrde's takeover of the [[Car'das/Legends|Car'das Smuggling Syndicate]]''[[Vision of the Future]]'', Aves was close to the smuggling chief, serving as his personal aide and advisor. Officially, Aves operated as the coordinator for field operatives and dispatch manager for the smuggling group''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]''. During the [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|Thrawn Crisis]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Aves was never very far from the smuggler chief. When [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Grand Admiral Thrawn]] arrived on [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] in search of [[Ysalamiri/Legends|ysalamiri]], Aves functioned as the organization's communications officer and quickly relayed intel regarding the Grand Admiral and the ''[[Chimaera/Legends|Chimaera]]'' directly to Karrde. After the Grand Admiral left Myrkr, Aves piloted the ''[[Wild Karrde]]'' for a standard mission. Aboard, [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] felt a disturbance in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], which caused Karrde to order Aves to redirect their flight. Mara's hunch lead the ''Wild Karrde'' to intercept Luke Skywalker's disabled [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|x-wing]]. The ''Wild Karrde's'' (female) comms officer negotiated the fee for Luke's rescue and Aves personally greeted Luke at the docking port. After introducing Luke to Captain Talon Karrde, Karrde and Luke briefly discussed Luke's relationship with Mara. Finding nothing of interest, Karrde apologized to Luke and gave the signal to subdue him. With the help of the [[Ysalamiri/Legends|Force-dampening field]] effect of the on-board ysalamiri, Aves was able to successfully incapacitate Luke Skywalker with a [[Stun baton/Legends|stun baton]][[Dark Force Rising 2|''Dark Force Rising'' 2]]. Back at the Myrkr base, while Mara Jade and Talon Karrde debated what to do with their guest, Aves received an in-orbit landing request from returning operative, [[Fynn Torve]]. Torve's communique indicated that he had guests in tow: [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], following a lead on potential allies. Karrde ordered Mara to hide Luke on base while he intercepted Han and Lando for dinner. At dinner, Aves received a subsequent unexpected hail from Grand Admiral Thrawn, aboard the ''Chimaera,'' already in near-orbit with the Myrkr base. While Karrde maneuvered all interested parties around the smuggling hideout and obfuscated each party from the others' presence, Luke escaped the base and fled into the surrounding forest. Sensing impending disaster, Mara followed Luke into the forest with a speeder. In a gesture of reciprocal assistance, Grand Admiral offered stormtrooper hunting parties to Karrde to retrieve the stolen speeder and suspect associates. In a race against the clock, Aves worked with Han Solo and Lando Calrissian to find and rescue Mara and Luke from [[Hyllyard City|Hyllard City]] before the Empire did. After the [[Battle of Sluis Van|Battle of Sulis Van]], Aves piloted the ''Wild Karrde'' on its final voyage from the Myrkr base''[[Dark Force Rising]]''. While Karrde took a last look at the forest-world, Aves hid the ship behind an orbiting asteroid. Unknown to Karrde or Aves, Grand Admiral Thrawn had been waiting for Talon Karrde to make a sentimental send-off, and prepared to attack the smuggler on exodus. Aves argued with Mara as she wrest the helm and pushed them into hyperspace -- saving them from ambush. This lead Aves to question Mara's loyalty to Karrde, which later resulted in Aves lying-in-wait to confront Mara after her apparent betrayal and Karrde's arrest by the Empire. Mara convinced Aves that she had been tricked by Grand Admiral Thrawn and that she intended to prove her loyalty by rescuing Karrde from imprisonment aboard the ''Chimaera''. Aves arranged to supply her with a [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|skipray]] and a ysalamiri [[nutrient frame]] for a mission to [[Jomark/Legends|Jomark]] and, instead of joining the rescue mission, chose to stay and finish clearing the crew from the compromised base[[Dark Force Rising 3|''Dark Force Rising'' 3]]. After the [[Battle for the Katana fleet|Battle for the Katana Fleet]], Aves and Karrde encountered long-time associate [[Samuel Tomas Gillespee|Samuel Tomas Gillespie]], aboard the ''Wild Karrde'' and ''[[Kern's Pride]],'' respectively. Pleasantries were interrupted as Gillespie's freighter was attacked by pirates. Aves and Karrde maneuvered the ''Wild Karrde'' into combat to provide the ''Kern's Pride'' an opportunity to escape[[The Last Command 2|''The Last Command'' 2]]. Neither ship fled the scene -- both smuggling organizations revealed they had backup in nearby space, for just such an encounter. This inspired to Karrde invite Gillespie to help him form the [[Smugglers' Alliance]], which Karrde, Gillespie, and Aves continue to plan and form throughout the following months''[[The Last Command]]''. Karrde and Gillespie arranged a meeting of top smugglers at the [[Whistler's Whirlpool Tapcafe]] on [[Trogan/Legends|Trogan]]. Karrde invited the gathered smuggling chiefs to join the burgeoning Smugglers' Alliance and make a move against the Empire by helping him discover where Grand Admiral Thrawn's clone army was being bred''[[The Last Command]]''. The smugglers argued to remain neutral in the war until the meeting was attacked by Imperials, resulting in [[Lishma]]'s death. The remaining leaders agreed to band together and fight against the Empire. During this and subsequent negotiations, Karrde trusted Aves with command of the crews of the [[Starry Ice|''Starry Ice'']], the ''[[Etherway]]'', and the ''Wild Karrde''. Some months after the Thrawn Crisis, Karrde gave Aves a command of his own: the ''[[Lastri's Ort]]''.''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' ==Personality and traits== As a primary go-between and point of contact for the crew, the crew often confided in Aves if they were uncomfortable speaking to Karrde himself. In turn, Aves provided Karrde with advice on the mood and morale of his employees. Aves would voice concerns on behalf of the crew — without naming names — to Karrde, secure in the knowledge that Karrde wouldn't use the information against them. Aves was loyal and trustworthy. He was unafraid to question orders and the validity of a plan, and would often follow through with an order regardless of the answer. Aves prioritized the safety of his crew and the organization above all—and was unafraid to draw a blaster on a suspected threat. When the ''Wild Karrde'' intercepted Luke Skywalker's disabled X-Wing in space, Aves was the one to run interference and subdue the Jedi Master. When Mara and Luke were at risk of being captured by Grand Admiral Thrawn's stormtrooper hunting party, Aves lead Lando and Han into the rescue mission. When Lando tried to subdue Aves, stalling for Luke to use the Force on a stone archway, and Aves pulled a blaster on him for being a suspected coward and jeopardizing the rescue attempt. After Mara's suspected betrayal and Karrde's arrest, Aves took point to move the remaining crew to safety and confronted Mara at blaster-point. Aves was also prompt into action to rescue Samuel Tomas Gillespie from pirates attacking the ''Kern's Pride'', and among the first to draw a blaster during the ambush on Togran. ==Powers and abilities== A competent navigator and freighter pilot, Aves was often trusted with command of several ships in Karrde's fleet. He was eventually granted his own command of the ''Lastri's Ort'', the largest warship in Karrde's organization''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]''. Aves was streetwise and spoke Basic and Huttese. He was knowledgeable about planetary systems, gambling and bargaining.''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' ==Behind the scenes== Aves' first appearance in [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] was as a communications officer on Talon Karrde's Myrkr base, in ''[[Heir to the Empire]]''. The ''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' describes him as "having served with Karrde since the early days of his operation." In February 2001, authors [[Tish Eggleston Pahl]] and [[Chris Cassidy]] retroactively describe Aves as "the new hire" to Karrde's organization, in their short story ''[[A Credit for Your Thoughts]]'', featured in ''[[Star Wars Gamer 2]]''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Aves}} *{{GamerCite|2|A Credit for Your Thoughts}} *''[[Heir to the Empire]]'' {{Ab|Heir to the Empire (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Heir to the Empire 4|''Heir to the Empire'' 4]] *''[[Dark Force Rising]]'' {{Ab|Dark Force Rising (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[Dark Force Rising 1|''Dark Force Rising'' 1]] *[[Dark Force Rising 2|''Dark Force Rising'' 2]] *''[[The Last Command]]'' {{Ab|The Last Command (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Heir to the Empire Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Thrawn Trilogy Sourcebook]]'' *{{GamerCite|3|The Smugglers Alliance}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|aves|Aves}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Aves (Schmuggler) |hu=Aves |fi=Aves }} [[Category:Car'das members]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Smugglers' Alliance members]]
